item.title: Relief operation stalls as govt reviews haor flood victim list
item.description: The government has suspended distribution of cash and food assistance among the families affected by the recent flash floods in the haor region with authorities still reviewing the list of beneficiaries....

item.title: MEASLES: 9 more die, 1,192 cases detected in 24 hours
item.description: At least nine more children died and 1,192 more were diagnosed with measles or showing measles symptoms in the 24 hours until 8:00am Tuesday....Politics

item.title: MEASLES: 9 more die, 1,192 cases detected in 24 hours
item.description: At least nine more children died and 1,192 more were diagnosed with measles or showing measles symptoms in the 24 hours until 8:00am Tuesday....

item.title: PADMA BARRAGE PROJECT: ECNEC likely to review proposal today
item.description: The executive committee of the National Economic Council is scheduled to review today a proposal on the first phase of the Padma Barrage project Bangladesh has long been contemplating to offset ecological damages caused by the controversial barrage across......Cultural Event Listings

item.title: US must accept peace plan: Iran
item.description: Iran’s chief negotiator said Tuesday that Washington must accept Tehran’s latest peace plan or face failure, after US president Donald Trump warned the truce in the Middle East war was on the brink of collapse....

item.title: 56 under-12 girls raped in four months
item.description: The recent rise in rapes of girls across the country has raised concerns as at least 56 under-12 girls were victims of the sexual crime in the first four months of 2026....

item.title: BB decides to liquidate five NBFIs by July
item.description: Bangladesh Bank on Tuesday decided to liquidate five non-bank financial institutions (NBFIs) as they sinks deeper into insolvency, mounting defaults and a prolonged depositor crisis......

item.title: BB reviewing loans above Tk 20 crore
item.description: The Bangladesh Bank has started reviewing all loans above Tk 20 crore disbursed by banks since July 2025 to date as part of a fresh crackdown on irregular lending amid rising concerns over bad loans, fund diversion and large-scale money laundering......

item.title: 39 banks form Tk 425cr fund to finance startups
item.description: Thirty-nine commercial banks have launched Bangladesh’s first institutionally backed venture capital platform with a committed fund of Tk 425 crore to finance local startups and reduce dependence on foreign investors......

item.title: BB mandates QR-based visa verification
item.description: The Bangladesh Bank has made mandatory the inclusion of Quick Response or QR codes in bank statements, solvency certificates and investment certificates submitted for visa applications so that embassies and visa centres can instantly verify the authenticity of financial documents digitally......

item.title: RSC recognises 46 RMG factories for workplace safety
item.description: The RMG Sustainability Council on Tuesday distributed formal recognition letter to 46 readymade garment factories for ensuring minimum life-safety standards in their workplaces......

item.title: Public subscription of Midland Bank Growth Fund closes
item.description: Public subscription of ‘Midland Bank Growth Fund’ has concluded, which is an open-ended mutual fund managed by Midland Bank Asset Management Company Limited......

item.title: DBH proposes 15pc dividend for 2025
item.description: DBH Finance PLC has proposed 15 per cent dividend for the financial year ending December 31, 2025, said a press release......

item.title: Lanka risks repeating cycle of corruption
item.description: THE death of the most important suspect in the SriLankan Airlines airbus deal has drawn intense public speculation. Kapila Chandrasena, the former CEO of the heavily loss-making national airline, was found dead under circumstances that the police are still investigating. He had recently been arrested by the Commission to Investigate Allegations of Bribery or......

item.title: Cycling may come to aid
item.description: DHAKA’S traffic has become a national headache. A recent study estimates that congestion now costs the city around Tk 37,000 crore every year, more than the construction cost of the Padma Bridge itself. Nearly five million working hours are reportedly lost annually. According to the World Bank, traffic congestion directly accounts for about 2.9 per cent of Bangladesh’s......Cultural Event Listings

item.title: Lead poison leaves life in lurch
item.description: IN THE crowded alleys of Dhaka, beside busy highways, in industrial belts and around informal recycling yards, a poison continues to spread quietly through air, soil, water and food. It accumulates slowly, enters homes invisibly, settles into children’s bodies and leaves behind damage that may last a lifetime. That poison is lead. Although it has for long been globally......

item.title: Time for smart governance
item.description: IN ALMOST every country, the education system is not a single, tidy arrangement but a layered, intricate field shaped by multiple authorities, competing priorities and diverse learners. Bangladesh is no exception. Its education landscape stretches across public and private institutions, madrassahs, NGO schools and both registered and unregistered providers. It operates......

item.title: No end to mob violence in sight
item.description: MOB violence, which increased after the fall of the Awami League regime, has continued unabated after the Bangladesh Nationalist Party assumed office. At least three were beaten to death by a mob on suspicion of theft in Gazipur on May 9. A robbery suspect was killed in a mob beating in Bogura on May 8. Rights group Ain O Salish Kendra data show that in the first quarter......Geographic Reference

item.title: Dhaka must assert its Ganges treaty stance to Delhi
item.description: INDIA’S present stance on the formula that effected the Ganges water sharing treaty signed with Bangladesh in 1996 appears to cast a shadow on the renewal of the agreement as it expires after 30 years in December. India’s foreign secretary is reported to have recently said that water has always been a priority among bilateral issues as the two countries share 54 rivers. India......

item.title: Cloth trader’s body found in city
item.description: The police recovered the body of a cloth trader from his rented house in the capital’s Noyabazar area of Bangshal on Tuesday....

item.title: Govt warns of action against extortion during Eid travel, cattle transport
item.description: Home minister Salahuddin Ahmed said on Tuesday that the government would take strict measures against extortion on roads and waterways during Eid-ul-Azha travel and the transport of sacrificial animals across the country....

item.title: ‘Settlement reached’ at Raiganj PS over alleged robbery
item.description: An allegation of robbery involving a former Bangladesh Nationalist Party leader in Sirajganj’s Raiganj upazila has been settled through a compromise at the Raiganj police station, with the complainant eventually withdrawing his written complaint....Diaspora Community Forum

item.title: Sundarban pirate gang ‘chief’ held with arms
item.description: The Bangladesh Coast Guard arrested Sundarban pirate gang chief Md Zahidul Islam alias Mejo Jahangir along with arms and ammunition during a special drive in Khulna’s Dacope upazila early Tuesday....

item.title: Uprising museum likely to open late July or early Aug
item.description: Prime minister Tarique Rahman is likely to inaugurate the July Uprising Memorial Museum at the Ganabhaban by the end of this month or on the first day of August....

item.title: Three held over rape of woman in Sylhet
item.description: The Rapid Action Battalion arrested three men from Sylhet on Tuesday on charges of raping a woman after they had allegedly allured her to marriage after recovering the victim....

item.title: Man, son stabbed dead in Dinajpur
item.description: A man and his son were stabbed to death allegedly by two of his sons from his second marriage over a dispute over property in Mondolpara area of Chirirbandar upazila of Dinajpur Tuesday morning....

item.title: BSCPLC denies bandwidth shortage, calls campaign misleading
item.description: The Bangladesh Submarine Cables PLC has dismissed concerns over any possible shortage of international bandwidth in Bangladesh, saying the country currently has sufficient capacity to meet both existing and future demand....

item.title: DB busts robber gang plotting pre-Eid heists
item.description: The Detective Brach of Dhaka Metropolitan Police busted a robber gang in the capital for allegedly planning robberies ahead of Eid-ul-Azha, one of the biggest religious festivals of Muslims, later this month....Maps

item.title: Auto-rickshaw driver’s throat-slit body recovered
item.description: The police recovered the throat-slit body of a teenage auto-rickshaw driver from the Ujarpara Mohar Bari area under Gacha police station in Gazipur early Tuesday....

item.title: NATIONAL PENSION AUTHORITY: Scheme for 4cr families by 2030 planned
item.description: The newly elected government led by the Bangladesh Nationalist Party has targeted bringing one person from each of four crore families under the pension scheme by 2030....

item.title: Vijay meets AIADMK leaders backing TVK
item.description: Ahead of the crucial floor test in the Tamil Nadu assembly on Wednesday, chief minister and TVK supremo Vijay met rebel AIADMK leaders CV Shanmugam and SP Velumani on Tuesday, reports India Today....

item.title: Pakistan suicide bombing kills nine
item.description: A suicide bomber in a three-wheel vehicle laden with explosives killed at least nine people and wounded 34 others in northwest Pakistan on Tuesday, police said....

item.title: No move from Delhi yet on border fencing: minister
item.description: Home minister Salahuddin Ahmed said on Tuesday that Bangladesh had not yet observed any fresh official move from the Indian central government regarding the construction of barbed wire fences along the border, and that any such issue would be addressed diplomatically if formally proposed....

item.title: Locals resist BSF attempt to seize cows
item.description: Bangladesh nationals in the Dhabolguri bordering area under Patgram upazila of Lalmonirhat district on Tuesday resisted the Indian Border Security Force’s attempt to seize cows of Bangladeshis along the border. ...Diaspora Community Forum

item.title: Israeli strikes killed 380 since truce: Lebanon
item.description: Israeli strikes on Lebanon have killed 380 people since a ceasefire in the Israel-Hezbollah war began on April 17, Lebanon’s health minister Rakan Nassereddine told a press conference on Tuesday....

item.title: SCBA election begins today without pro-AL candidates
item.description: The two-day election to the executive committee of the Supreme Court Bar Association is going to begin on Wednesday without any candidate backed by the Awami League, activities of which are now suspended....

item.title: 72 BU teachers resign from admin posts
item.description: Seventy-two teachers of Barishal University resigned from various administrative posts, including those of proctor, provosts and chairpersons of all 25 departments, on Monday and Tuesday to press home their demand for promotions....

item.title: Jane Fonda, Gong Li open Cannes Film Festival
item.description: US cinema veteran Jane Fonda and Chinese star Gong Li opened the 79th Cannes Film Festival on Tuesday in front of a celebrity-studded audience…...

item.title: BRAC aims to create 1m jobs in 5 years
item.description: BRAC has announced plans to create employment opportunities for 1 million people over the next five years as part of its new strategic priorities aimed at supporting women, youth and marginalised communities across Bangladesh....Diaspora Community Forum

item.title: NSU holds consultation on occupational safety course
item.description: The EHS+ Centre of North South University, in collaboration with the International Labour Organization, organised a Consultation Programme on the campus in Dhaka on May 4....

item.title: BMU urges global-standard nursing on World Nurses Day
item.description: Bangladesh Medical University on Tuesday celebrated the World Nurses Day 2026 through rallies, discussions, tree plantation and cultural programmes on its campus....

item.title: Rano never deviated from political ideals: discussion
item.description: Politicians at a commemoration meeting in Dhaka on Tuesday said that left-wing politician and author Haider Akbar Khan Rano never strayed from hispoliticalideals in his long political career....Maps

item.title: Netherlands delegation discusses maritime cooperation with CPA
item.description: A high-level delegation from the Netherlands on Tuesday held a courtesy meeting with officials of the Chittagong Port Authority to discuss maritime cooperation, sustainable transport and future port development initiatives....

item.title: Last respects paid to thespian Ataur Rahman
item.description: People from all walks of life paid their last respects to Independence Award and Ekushey Padak-winning theatre exponent Ataur Rahman at a mourning ceremony held at the Central Shaheed Minar in Dhaka on Tuesday....
